note: Confirmation follow-ups for LLM-detected free-text signals (issue #26). One per hard-coded red-flag type. The AI only proposes showing one of these; the caregiver's own yes/no/not-sure answer is what the deterministic red-flag rules read (a confirmed 'yes' on FU_<type> triggers the matching rule). Wordings are clinical content — advisor sign-off required before release.

follow_ups.text: Thinking about what you wrote — has [child] lost words or skills they used to have?
follow_ups.hint: Only choose yes if that matches what you meant. Your answer here is what counts, not our reading of it.

follow_ups.text: You mentioned something about how [child] responds. When you call [child]'s name, do they usually not react, even after a few tries?
follow_ups.hint: Take your time — "I'm not sure" is always okay.

follow_ups.text: From what you wrote — does [child] currently have no way to show you what they want, with words, sounds, gestures, or pointing?
follow_ups.hint: Every child communicates differently. Answer for how things are right now.

follow_ups.text: You wrote something we want to be sure we understood. Does [child] do things that hurt their own body, like hitting their head or biting themselves?
follow_ups.hint: This helps us point you to the right support. You know [child] best.

follow_ups.text: About what you wrote on eating — does [child] eat so few foods that you worry about their growth or health?
follow_ups.hint: Lots of children are picky eaters. Choose yes only if it truly worries you.

follow_ups.text: About what you wrote on sleep — are [child]'s sleep struggles serious enough to affect the whole family most nights?
follow_ups.hint: Answer for a typical week, not the odd hard night.

follow_ups.text: You mentioned a change. Has [child]'s behaviour changed suddenly and noticeably in recent weeks?
follow_ups.hint: A gradual shift over months is different — this asks about a sudden change.

follow_ups.text: From what you wrote — are you worried about [child]'s safety, or the safety of people around them?
follow_ups.hint: Sharing this helps us show you the right next steps sooner.
